name: New Mexico Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Nutrition and Dietetics Practice Board
provider.parentOrganization: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department

address.description: RLD - Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Board, P.O. Box 25101, Santa Fe, NM 87504

renewalCycle: Every 1 year + 15 CE hours or active CDR

requiredDocuments.name: Copy of current CDR registration card
Background check
requiredDocuments.description: Must be conducted within the past 90 days per 16.14.3.8 NMAC.

requiredDocuments.name: Official academic transcript
requiredDocuments.description: Must be sent directly from the college or university to the Board.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a New Mexico dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost for a New Mexico dietitian license is $200, broken down as a $50 non-refundable application fee plus a $150 initial license fee. Annual renewal costs $75.

mainEntity.name: Is a background check required for a New Mexico dietitian license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Per current NMAC regulations, a background check conducted within the past 90 days is required for initial dietitian and nutritionist licensure.

mainEntity.name: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New Mexico?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take Varies (~2 Weeks).

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
